enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall put in place Board approved credit policy covering, inter alia, the areas
specified below, to the extent such activities are undertaken by it.
enriched_requirements.object: approved credit policy covering, inter alia, the areas specified below, to the extent such activities are undertaken by it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence requirements with respect to LSPs
(1) Digital lending by an AIFI involving a LSP, shall be carried out under a contractual
agreement between the AIFI and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective
roles, rights, and obligations of each party thereto.
enriched_requirements.object: a contractual agreement between the AIFI and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall conduct enhanced due diligence before they enter into an agreement
with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical capabilities,
robustness of data privacy policies and storage systems, fairness in conduct with
borrowers, past records of conduct and ability to comply with all applicable
regulations and statutes.
enriched_requirements.object: before they enter into an agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical capabilities, r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An AIFI shall carry out periodic review of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the terms
of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event of any
deviation therefrom.
enriched_requirements.object: of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) An AIFI shall lay down, as part of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for the
loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs.
enriched_requirements.object: of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) An AIFI shall impart necessary guidance to LSP acting as a recovery agent, to
discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the
applicable instructions in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions-
Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025, and other relevant instructions
as issued from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: LSP acting as a recovery agent, to discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the applicable in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) An AIFI shall continue to conform to the extant guidelines on outsourcing in
directions Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Managing Risks
in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and shall ensure that the LSPs engaged by them
and the DLAs (either of the AIFI or of the LSPs engaged by the AIFI) comply with
these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: the extant guidelines on outsourcing in directions Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Managing Risks in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) As an overarching principle, any outsourcing agreement entered into by an AIFI
with an LSP shall in no manner dilute or absolve the AIFI of its obligations under
any statutory or regulatory provision, and the AIFI shall remain fully responsible
and liable for all acts and omissions of the LSP.

(2) LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's

The name of the

borrowers and products.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The mechanism adopted by the LSP and any subsequent
changes to this mechanism shall be properly documented.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Assessing the borrower's creditworthiness
(1) An AIFI shall obtain the necessary information relating to economic profile of the
borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before extending
any loan, including, at a minimum, age, occupation and income details.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Loan disbursal, servicing and repayment
(1) Disbursement of loan by an AIFI shall always be made into the bank account of the
borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory
mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator), flow of money between
lenders for co-lending transactions and disbursals for specific end use, provided
the loan is disbursed directly into the bank account of the end-beneficiary.
enriched_requirements.object: the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator), flow of money between lenders for co-lending transactions and disbursals for specific end use

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFI
shall ensure that in no case, disbursal is made to a third-party account, including
the accounts of LSP, except as provided for in this Chapter.

bank account of the borrower, but the repayment is from the corporate employer,
can be allowed subject to the condition that the loan is repaid by the corporate
employer by deducting the amount from the borrower's salary.

However, it must be
ensured that LSPs do not have any control over the flow of funds directly or
indirectly in such transactions and that repayment is directly from the bank account

(All India Financial Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions,
2025, subject to the condition that no third party other than the lenders in a co-
lending transaction shall have direct or indirect control over the flow of funds at any
point of time.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) An AIFI shall ensure that all loan servicing, repayment, etc.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The flow of funds between the bank accounts of the borrower and the AIFI shall
not be controlled either directly or indirectly by a third-party, including the LSP.
enriched_requirements.object: or indirectly by a third-party, including the LSP

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (4) An AIFI shall ensure that any fees, charges, reimbursements, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: charges, reimbursements, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of delinquent loans, AIFI may deploy physical interface to recover loans in
cash, wherever necessary.
enriched_requirements.object: recover loans in cash

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, any recovery by cash shall be duly reflected in full
in the borrower's account on the same day and AIFI shall ensure that any fees,
charges, etc., payable to LSPs for such recovery are paid directly by the AIFI and
are not charged by LSP to the borrower either directly or indirectly from the
recovery proceeds.
enriched_requirements.object: full in the borrower's account on the same day and AIFI shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Cooling-off period
(1) The borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit a digital loan by paying the
principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial "cooling-
off period".
enriched_requirements.object: option to exit a digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial "cooling- o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling off period shall be determined by the AIFI as part of their
credit policy, subject to the period so determined not being less than one day.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI as part of their credit policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For
borrower continuing with the loan even after cooling-off period, pre-payment shall
continue to be allowed as per the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: as per the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI may retain a reasonable one-time processing fee, if the customer exits the
loan during the cooling-off period.
enriched_requirements.object: processing fee

conditions: if applicable
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This, if applicable, shall be disclosed to the
customer upfront in KFS.
enriched_requirements.object: customer upfront in KFS

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Grievance redressal
(1) An AIFI, and their LSP which has an interface with the borrower, shall designate
nodal grievance redressal officers to deal with digital lending related complaints/
issues raised by the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: officers to deal with digital lending related complaints/ issues raised by the borrower

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Contact details of the nodal grievance redressal officers shall be prominently
displayed on the websites of the AIFI, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in the
KFS provided to the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: the websites of the AIFI, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in the KFS provided to the borrower

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The facility of lodging complaint shall also be made available on the DLA and on
the website as stated above.
enriched_requirements.object: on the DLA and on the website as stated above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It is reiterated that responsibility of grievance
redressal shall continue to remain with the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This information shall be
suitably conveyed to the borrower.
enriched_requirements.object: the borrower

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Collection, usage and sharing of data with third parties
(1) An AIFI shall ensure that any collection of data by their DLA and DLA of their LSP
is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having audit trail.

In any case, AIFI shall also ensure that DLA of AIFI/LSP desist from accessing
mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony
functions, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: of AIFI/LSP desist from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony functions, etc

conditions: if required
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The borrower shall be provided with an option to give or deny consent for use of
specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke consent
already granted to collect personal data and if required, make the AIFI/LSP delete/
forget the data.
enriched_requirements.object: option to give or deny consent for use of specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke consent alre

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Explicit consent of the borrower shall be taken before sharing personal information
with any third party, except for cases where such sharing is required as per
statutory or regulatory requirement.
enriched_requirements.object: personal information with any third party

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Storage of data
(1) An AIFI shall ensure that LSP engaged by them do not store personal information
of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address, contact details
of the customer, etc.) that may be required to carry out their operations or service
within the scope of the AIFI-LSP agreement.
enriched_requirements.object: by them do not store personal information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibility regarding data privacy
and security of the customer's personal information on an ongoing basis shall be
that of the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall ensure that clear policy guidelines regarding the storage of customer
data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time for which data
can be stored, restrictions on the use of data, data destruction protocol, standards
for handling security breach, etc., are put in place and also disclosed by the AIFI
and the LSP engaged by the RE prominently on their website and DLA at all times.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines regarding the storage of customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time for which

unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: data is stored/ collected by the AIFI and LSP

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions.
enriched_requirements.object: is stored only in servers located within India, while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in

enriched_requirements.object: outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of processing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Comprehensive privacy policy
(1) An AIFI and LSPs engaged by the AIFI shall have a comprehensive privacy policy
compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and the Reserve Bank
guidelines which shall be made available publicly on the website of the AIFI and
LSP, as the case may be.

enriched_requirements.object: the privacy policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Technology standards

technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by the Reserve

undertaking digital lending.

CIC Regulations, 2006 and related guidelines issued by

AIFI shall ensure
that LSPs, if any, associated with such deferred payment credit products shall
abide by the extant outsourcing guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank and be
guided by these Directions.

requisite format as given in the Annex-I to these Directions.

Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: DLAs or any associated entity as conferring any form of registration, authorization, or endorsement by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AIFI shall also ensure that such inclusion is not misrepresented in
any marketing, promotional, or other materials issued by or on behalf of the DLAs.
enriched_requirements.object: inclusion is not misrepresented in any marketing, promotional, or other materials issued by or on behalf of the DLAs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Eligibility as Default Loss Guarantee (DLG) provider
(1) An AIFI may enter into DLG arrangements only with an LSP / other lender engaged
as an LSP.
enriched_requirements.object: only with an LSP / other lender engaged as an LSP

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the LSP providing DLG shall be incorporated as a company
under the Companies Act, 2013.
enriched_requirements.object: company under the Companies Act, 2013

conditions: if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence and other requirements with respect to DLG provider
(1) An AIFI, including an AIFI acting as DLG provider, shall lay down, as part of its
policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover,
process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the
fees, if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider, as the case may be, before
entering into any DLG arrangement.
enriched_requirements.object: of its policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover, process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the fees

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall ensure that any DLG arrangement does not act as a substitute for
credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to be
put in place irrespective of the DLG cover.
enriched_requirements.object: arrangement does not act as a substitute for credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to be put

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Every time an AIFI enters into or renews a DLG arrangement, it shall obtain
adequate information to satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able
to honour it.
enriched_requirements.object: satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able to honour it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such information shall, at a minimum, include a declaration from the
DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider, on the
aggregate DLG amount outstanding, the number of lenders and the respective
number of portfolios against which DLG has been provided.
enriched_requirements.object: include a declaration from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider, on the aggregate DLG amount outst

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The declaration shall
also contain past default rates on similar portfolios.
enriched_requirements.object: rates on similar portfolios

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Restrictions on entering into DLG arrangements
(1) An AIFI shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities offered
through digital lending channel.
enriched_requirements.object: for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An AIFI shall not enter into DLG arrangements on the loans which are covered by
the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: on the loans which are covered by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Structure of DLG arrangements
(1) DLG arrangements shall be backed by an explicit and legally enforceable contract
between the AIFI and the DLG provider.
enriched_requirements.object: explicit and legally enforceable contract between the AIFI and the DLG provider

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such contract, among other things, shall
contain the following details:

Forms of DLG
(1) AIFI shall accept DLG only in one or more of the following forms:
enriched_requirements.object: one or more of the following forms:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Cap on DLG
(1) An AIFI shall ensure that the total amount of DLG cover on any outstanding portfolio
which is specified upfront shall not exceed five per cent of the total amount
disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time.
enriched_requirements.object: of the total amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time

conditions:
In case of implicit guarantee
arrangements
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of implicit guarantee
arrangements, the DLG Provider shall not bear performance risk of more than the
equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio.
enriched_requirements.object: more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The portfolio over which DLG can be offered shall consist of identifiable and
measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set').
enriched_requirements.object: measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This portfolio
shall remain fixed for the purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be dynamic.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be dynamic

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This portfolio shall
remain "frozen" for the purpose of the specific DLG arrangement - meaning that no
loan assets can be added or removed from it, except through loan repayment/
write-off.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose of the specific DLG arrangement - meaning that no loan assets can be added or removed from it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ceiling for DLG cover on such portfolio shall be fixed at ₹2 crore (5 per cent of
₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the loans
are disbursed.
enriched_requirements.object: crore (5 per cent of ₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the loans are disbursed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Then as on April 1, 2024, the DLG cover available for the portfolio
shall be ₹0.5 crore (5 per cent of disbursed).

conditions: if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequently, if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15, 2024, the
DLG cover shall proportionately increase to ₹1 crore effective April 15, 2024.
enriched_requirements.object: crore effective April 15, 2024

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In
this case, the outstanding portfolio in the books of the RE would be ₹15 crore and
the DLG cover shall remain at ₹1 crore.

In this case, as of
Sept 30, 2024 the outstanding portfolio in the books of the AIFI shall be ₹15 crore
(₹20 crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured without default) but no
headroom for DLG will be available as the maximum permissible DLG cover of ₹1
crore (5 per cent of disbursed) has been exhausted.
enriched_requirements.object: crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured without default) but no headroom for DLG will be available as the maximum permi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In such a case, the
amount of the outstanding portfolio in the books of the RE as on October 31, 2024
shall come down to ₹14 crore (₹20 crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans
matured without any default less ₹1 crore loans which were in default and
recovered).
enriched_requirements.object: crore (₹20 crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured without any default less ₹1 crore loans which were in default and rec

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Recognition of NPA
(1) Recognition of individual loan assets in the portfolio as Non-Performing Asset
(NPA) and consequent provisioning shall be the responsibility of the AIFI as per
the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition,
Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025 irrespective of any DLG
cover available at the portfolio level.
enriched_requirements.object: the AIFI as per the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Pr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The amount of DLG invoked shall not be set off against the underlying individual
loans, i.e.
enriched_requirements.object: the underlying individual loans, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
the liability of the borrowers in respect of the underlying loan shall remain
unaffected.

(4) DLG amount once invoked by the AIFI shall not be reinstated, including through
loan recovery.
enriched_requirements.object: loan recovery

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of DLG for regulatory capital
(1) Capital computation, i.e., computation of exposure and application of Credit Risk
Mitigation benefits on individual loan assets in the portfolio shall continue to be
governed by Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: by Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In case, DLG provider is an AIFI, it shall deduct full amount of the DLG which is
outstanding from its capital.
enriched_requirements.object: the DLG which is outstanding from its capital

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Invocation and tenor of DLG
(1) An AIFI shall invoke DLG within a maximum overdue period of 120 days, unless
the loan dues are made good by the borrower before that.
enriched_requirements.object: maximum overdue period of 120 days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The period for which the DLG agreement remains in force shall not be less than
the longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio.
enriched_requirements.object: longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Disclosure requirements
(1) An AIFI shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that LSPs with whom they have
a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of portfolios and
the respective amount of each portfolio on which DLG has been offered.
enriched_requirements.object: mechanism to ensure that LSPs with whom they have a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of portfolios and t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The name
of the lender(s) may or may not be disclosed as part of disclosure under this
provision.
enriched_requirements.object: disclosed as part of disclosure under this provision

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Disclosure under para (1) above shall be made on a monthly basis, with the
disclosure for any given month to be provided no later than seven working days
following the conclusion of that month.
enriched_requirements.object: monthly basis, with the disclosure for any given month to be provided no later than seven working days following the conclusion of that m

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Exceptions
Guarantees covered under the following schemes/ entities shall not be covered within
the definition of DLG:
(1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small
Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income
Housing (CRGFTLIH) and individual schemes under National Credit Guarantee
Trustee Company Ltd (NCGTC).
enriched_requirements.object: definition of DLG: (1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Gu

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
DLG arrangements entered between an AIFI and their LSP conforming to the
instructions laid down in this Chapter shall neither be treated as "synthetic
securitisation" as defined under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Securitisation Transactions) Directions, 2025, nor attract the provisions
of 'loan participation' as defined under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions- Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: "synthetic securitisation" as defined under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Securitisation Transac

conditions:
where financial
closure has been achieved as on October 1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The directions contained in this Chapter shall not apply to projects where financial
closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025 (the 'Effective Date' for this Chapter)
for which the prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing before October 1,
2025, which otherwise shall be treated as repealed, shall apply.
enriched_requirements.object: financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025 (the 'Effective Date' for this Chapter) for which the prudential gui

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, any
resolution of a fresh credit event and / or change in material terms and conditions in
the loan contract in such projects, subsequent to the effective date, shall be as per the
guidelines under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution
of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of application of prudential guidelines contained in this Chapter,
Projects shall be broadly divided into three phases namely:
(1) Design phase - This is the first phase which starts with the genesis of the project
and includes, inter-alia, designing, planning, obtaining all applicable clearances /
approvals till its financial closure.
enriched_requirements.object: three phases namely: (1) Design phase - This is the first phase which starts with the genesis of the project and includes, int

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy of an AIFI shall incorporate suitable clauses for sanction of project
finance exposures, taking into account inter alia the provisions under the directions in
this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: sanction of project finance exposures, taking into account inter alia the provisions under the directions in this Cha

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For all projects financed by the AIFI, it shall be ensured that:
(1) Financial closure has been achieved and original DCCO is clearly spelt out and
documented prior to disbursement of funds.
enriched_requirements.object: Financial closure has been achieved and original DCCO is clearly spelt out and documented prior to disbursement of funds

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the original or revised repayment tenor, including the moratorium
period, if any, shall not exceed 85 per cent of the economic life of a project.
enriched_requirements.object: of the economic life of a project

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For a given project, original / extended / actual DCCO, as the case may be, shall be
same across all lenders to the project.
enriched_requirements.object: lenders to the project

conditions:
where the aggregate exposure of the lenders is up to
₹1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In under-construction projects where the aggregate exposure of the lenders is up to
₹1,500 crore, no individual AIFI shall have an exposure which is less than 10 per cent
of the aggregate exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: is less than 10 per cent of the aggregate exposure

conditions:
where aggregate exposure of all lenders is
more than ₹1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders is
more than ₹1,500 crore, the exposure floor for an individual AIFI shall be 5 per cent or
₹150 crore, whichever is higher.
enriched_requirements.object: or ₹150 crore, whichever is higher

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, the above minimum exposure requirements shall not apply post-actual
DCCO and AIFI may freely acquire from or sell exposures to other lenders, in
compliance with guidelines contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI may freely acquire from or sell exposures to other lenders, in compliance with guidelines contained in the Reserve Ban

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Prior to actual
DCCO, lenders may acquire from or sell exposures to other lenders under a
syndication arrangement (as specified under Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025), provided the
share of individual AIFI is in adherence to the above limits.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures to other lenders under a syndication arrangement (as specified under Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall ensure that all applicable approvals / clearances for implementing /
constructing the project are obtained before financial closure.
enriched_requirements.object: approvals / clearances for implementing / constructing the project are obtained before financial closure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Approvals / clearances which are contingent upon achievement of certain milestones
in terms of project completion shall be deemed to be applicable only when such
milestones are achieved.
enriched_requirements.object: applicable only when such milestones are achieved

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Hence, the same shall not be treated as an
applicable mandatory pre-requisite at the time of financial closure.
enriched_requirements.object: applicable mandatory pre-requisite at the time of financial closure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall ensure availability of sufficient land / right of way for all projects before
disbursement of funds, subject to the following minimum requirements:
(1) For infrastructure projects under PPP model - 50 per cent
(2) For all other projects (non-PPP infrastructure, and non-infrastructure including CRE
& CRE-RH) - 75 per cent
(3) For transmission line projects - as decided by the AIFI
enriched_requirements.object: land / right of way for all projects before disbursement of funds

conditions: In case of infrastructure projects under PPP model
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of infrastructure projects under PPP model, disbursement of funds shall begin
only after declaration of the Appointed Date or its equivalent, for the project.
enriched_requirements.object: of the Appointed Date or its equivalent, for the project

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However,
in cases where non-fund based credit facilities may be mandated by the concession
granting authority as a pre-requisite for declaration of appointed date, the AIFI may
sanction such credit facilities, in adherence with the instructions on non-fund based
facilities as prescribed in Chapter V of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: concession granting authority as a pre-requisite for declaration of appointed date, the AIFI may sanction such credit facilities, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in respect of the exposures mentioned at paragraph 42 above, the original
DCCO documented in the financial closure document shall be modified to reflect any
change in the 'Appointed Date' by the concession granting authority prior to
disbursement of funds by way of a supplementary agreement between a lender and
the debtor subject to reassessment of project viability and obtention of sanction from
appropriate authorities.
enriched_requirements.object: any change in the 'Appointed Date' by the concession granting authority prior to disbursement of funds by way of a supplementar

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A Techno-Economic Viability (TEV) study shall be required for
this purpose for all projects where the aggregate exposure of all lenders is ₹100 crore
or more.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose for all projects where the aggregate exposure of all lenders is ₹100 crore or more

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall ensure that disbursal is proportionate to the stages of completion of the
project as also to the progress in equity infusion and other sources of finance, agreed
as part of financial closure and receipt of remaining applicable clearances.
enriched_requirements.object: proportionate to the stages of completion of the project as also to the progress in equity infusion and other sources of fina

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The lender's
Independent Engineer (LIE) / Architect shall certify the stages of completion of the
project.
enriched_requirements.object: completion of the project

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A project finance account may be classified as NPA during any time before
actual DCCO as per record of recovery, in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (All India
Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning)
Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: during any time before actual DCCO as per record of recovery, in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institut

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Creation and Maintenance of Database - Project specific data, in electronic and easily
accessible format, shall be captured and maintained by the AIFI on an ongoing basis.
enriched_requirements.object: by the AIFI on an ongoing basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A list of the relevant parameters which shall form part of project finance database, at a
minimum, is given below.
enriched_requirements.object: finance database, at a minimum, is given below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall update any change in parameters of a project finance exposure at the
earliest, but not later than 15 days from such change.
enriched_requirements.object: parameters of a project finance exposure at the earliest, but not later than 15 days from such change

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The necessary system in this
regard shall be put in place within three months of the effective date.
enriched_requirements.object: within three months of the effective date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make appropriate disclosures in their financial statements, under 'Notes
to Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions
- Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: their financial statements, under 'Notes to Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Within this Chapter, the term Regulated Entity (RE) or Regulated Entities (REs) shall
refer to:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks).
enriched_requirements.object: Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The directions in this Chapter shall not apply to the derivative exposures of the AIFI
(wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI), other than
the General Conditions as laid down in this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: exposures of the AIFI (wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI), other than the General Condition

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy of the AIFI shall incorporate suitable provisions for issue of NFB
facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted,
credit appraisal, security requirement, fraud prevention, overall monitoring mechanism
including post-sanction monitoring, delegation matrix, audit and internal controls,
compliance to uniform standards issued by standard setting bodies and other
safeguards.
enriched_requirements.object: issue of NFB facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted, credit a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall issue a NFB facility only on behalf of a customer having funded credit
facility from the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: only on behalf of a customer having funded credit facility from the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that this clause shall not be applicable in respect of:
(1) Derivative contracts entered into by the AIFI with counterparty.
enriched_requirements.object: of: (1) Derivative contracts entered into by the AIFI with counterparty

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The eligible financial collateral specified herein shall be as defined
under Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential Norms on
Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall not issue a NFB facility to any entity assuring redemption / repayment
of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any other form,
unless specifically permitted under any regulatory guidelines / directions issued by the
Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: to any entity assuring redemption / repayment of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any other form

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Once a NFB facility devolves and is converted into a fund based facility, then the
prudential norms shall be as applicable to fund based facilities.
enriched_requirements.object: fund based facilities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In general, a guarantee (or a counter-guarantee) issued by the AIFI (guarantor) shall
be irrevocable (i.e., there shall be no clause in the contract that would allow the
guarantor to unilaterally cancel the same), unconditional (i.e., there shall be no clause
in the contract that could prevent the bank from being obliged to pay out in a timely
manner in the event that the original counterparty fails to meet its obligation),
incontrovertible and shall contain a clear mechanism for honouring the same without
demur as and when invoked.

The AIFI shall put in place suitable internal aggregate / individual ceilings for issuance
of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular.
enriched_requirements.object: internal aggregate / individual ceilings for issuance of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of the internal policy relating to guarantees shall, inter alia, address
aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee /
commission / applicable charges, timelines for release of security, renewal, fraud
prevention measures etc.
enriched_requirements.object: aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee / commission / applicable charges, timelines fo

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall honour the guarantee issued by it as and when invoked in accordance
with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is court order
restraining the same.
enriched_requirements.object: by it as and when invoked in accordance with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is court order re

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever the AIFI issues an electronic Guarantee, it shall frame a standard operating
procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention;
enriched_requirements.object: procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
SOP shall, inter alia, consider the aspects mentioned from paragraphs 60 to 74 of
these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: the aspects mentioned from paragraphs 60 to 74 of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall have suitable enabling provisions in its credit policy which shall, inter
alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to be put in
place, delegation of authority, the monitoring process, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: in its credit policy which shall, inter alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall put in place appropriate SOPs for user reference, detailing all the steps
to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle.
enriched_requirements.object: SOPs for user reference, detailing all the steps to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Electronic Guarantees
shall not be issued without ensuring that the underlying transaction has been duly
reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS) / Trade Finance System (TFS).
enriched_requirements.object: that the underlying transaction has been duly reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS) / Trade Finance System (TFS)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall have a strong control environment covering the policies, processes and
systems;
enriched_requirements.object: environment covering the policies, processes and systems

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall ensure that all features relating to the entire lifecycle events of electronic
Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation etc.
enriched_requirements.object: relating to the entire lifecycle events of electronic Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
shall be
available on its platform through suitable integration with the electronic Guarantee
service provider.
enriched_requirements.object: platform through suitable integration with the electronic Guarantee service provider

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The CBS / TFS shall be integrated with the APIs and other related messaging platforms
offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in Straight Through Processing
(STP) mode, without any manual intervention.
enriched_requirements.object: APIs and other related messaging platforms offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in Straight Through Processing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall have an efficient system of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for issuance
and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access control and an
effective segregation of the role and accountability.
enriched_requirements.object: of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for issuance and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access control

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
No role involved in electronic Guarantee issuance lifecycle shall violate principle of
segregation of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall be allocated roles
/ privileges across systems, applications that are conflicting in nature or in violation of
four/ six eye principle.
enriched_requirements.object: of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall be allocated roles / privileges across systems, application

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The system access shall be provided only to specified users, and access through
generic user IDs shall not be permitted.

User review shall be continuous, at defined
periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with respective rights and privileges.
enriched_requirements.object: periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with respective rights and privileges

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The user privileges shall be decided on "need to know/ need to do" basis.
enriched_requirements.object: to know/ need to do" basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The AIFI shall have in place a system of periodical review and reconciliation of all the
electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the specified period.
enriched_requirements.object: system of periodical review and reconciliation of all the electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the specified pe

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The issuance of electronic Guarantees shall be mandatorily covered within the scope
of concurrent audit and RBIA of the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: the scope of concurrent audit and RBIA of the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The robustness of the electronic Guarantee systems shall be part of the Vulnerability
Assessment / Penetration Testing (VA / PT), Information Systems Audit.
